successCriteria.description: src/modules/audit/audit.model.ts exists and exports AuditRecord with fields id, entityType, entityId, action and exports CreateAuditRecordInput with fields entityType, entityId, action.
src/modules/audit/audit.repository.ts exists and exports AuditRepository with create and findByEntity methods using AuditRecord and CreateAuditRecordInput types from audit.model.ts.
src/modules/audit/audit.repository.ts exports an abstract PostgreSqlAuditRepository class implementing the AuditRepository contract with matching method signatures.
Repository contracts use types imported from src/modules/audit/audit.model.ts and introduce no circular dependencies.
tests/unit/modules/audit/audit.repository.spec.ts exists, uses Vitest, and verifies audit contract exports and TypeScript type compatibility.
The canonical audit_records schema is represented by the contract design with columns id, entity_type, entity_id, and action matching the specified SQL schema.

constraints: Limit implementation to audit persistence contracts only.
Create only the audit domain model, repository interfaces, PostgreSQL-facing repository contract abstraction, and unit test artifacts required by this phase.
Repository pattern: all database access must be defined through repository interfaces; do not perform direct database access in this phase.
AuditRecord is isolated and reusable for later workflow services.
Repository method signatures must use AuditRecord and CreateAuditRecordInput types imported from src/modules/audit/audit.model.ts.
No circular dependencies between audit model and repository contracts.
Use TypeScript, Node.js 20 project conventions, and Vitest for unit tests.
Audit records are a platform-wide requirement, but this phase defines contracts only and does not implement audit-writing behavior.
No API endpoints, RBAC logic, input-validation handlers, logging behavior, or async workflow implementations should be added in this phase.
Error-handling, logging, and persistence implementation details are out of scope because only interfaces/contracts are being created.
outOfScope: Leave approval or rejection workflow services.
Fastify routes, controllers, or API endpoints.
LeaveBalance domain models, repositories, or balance adjustment logic.
Notification domain models, repositories, or workflow processing.
Concrete PostgreSQL repository implementation and database access code.
Database migrations or schema deployment artifacts beyond documenting the contract schema.
Any files outside the audit module contracts and the specified audit unit test.
